source_paragraph: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.

The motivation stems from the challenges faced by auditors when identifying essential activities aligned with strategy of a company, often hindered by manual COBIT 2019 guideline navigation. The methodology involved creating interconnected nodes from COBIT 2019 Element in Neo4J. source_paragraph: In our case, quasi-continuous transmission is used through proper scheduling of OFDM transmission and echoes reception to prevent the coupling. • The receiver that continuously receives signal echoes coming from multiple targets must be able to distinguish which targets are very near or very far from the radar. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . source_paragraph: presented a novel multi-head attention mechanism that uses various atten- tion functions to capture diverse contextual elements. The multi-head attention approach allows attention to be simul- taneously allocated to information from different subspaces and representational positions. In contrast to soft attention, which focuses attention softly on the entire input to produce a context representation with weights determined based on the relevance of each part of the input, soft attention relies on only one attention mechanism. Kumar et al. developed the multi-head attention- based BiLSTM (MHA-BiLSTM) model. The results are quite promising compared to the use of single-head attention. source_paragraph: DATASET The DEAP dataset has 32 participants with a composition of 50% male and 50% female samples . The stimulus used is a video with a duration of 60 seconds. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. If the predicted label is lower than the true label, we define it False Positive (FP). Conversely, if the true label is lower than the predicted label, we define it False Negative (FN). In this context, having less FP is more important than having less FN, as the task objective is anomaly severity detection, where we aim to detect all abnormalities along with the severity level. Therefore, undetected abnormalities, or detecting higher severity as lower ones, indicate that the model fails to meet the task’s objectives. Despite the promising results, the model’s generality remains untested as the training dataset contains only log messages acquired from 82 drone models, mostly from DJI. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
